General Services Office (GSO), being the property and supply unit, for proper
and complete documentation and recording of the goods or items donated to
the City;
 require the Department/Office assigned in the distribution of the in-kind
donations to coordinate with the City GSO for proper control and monitoring
of the distributed and remaining items as of a given period;
 require the City GSO, in coordination with the appropriate City
Department/Office to prepare a Report/Summary/List of Donations Received,
Distributed and Balances; and
 instruct the City Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial
Statements the relevant information regarding the receipt, issuance and
balances of in-kind donations pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the City Mayor direct the
City Development Council to:
a) evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects, especially those in

that will cater to the present needs of the constituents;

the development projects that were needed by them before submitting to the
council to avoid various reprogramming; and

during the year in order to attain the desired socio-economic development
and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction

community.

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point

appropriation for the payment of insurance premiums of all insurable assets

 direct the City Accountant and City Treasurer to provide necessary

We reiterated our recommendation that the City Mayor instruct the City

We also recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a) City Accountant to verify and address the differences that exist between the
amounts set up for RPT Receivables and SET Receivables accounts as well
as their respective collections, and make the necessary adjustments; and
henceforth, always ensure the equality of amounts set up and collected for
RPT and SET Receivables;

b) City Accountant and the City Treasurer to reconcile the balances of
RPT/SET Receivables; and

c) City Treasurer to facilitate the enhancement of the RPT System to expedite
the preparation of the required list of taxpayers showing the correct amount
of tax due and collectible that will serve as the basis for setting up of the
RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the:

a) City Accountant and the OIC-City General Services Officer (GSO) to strictly
adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for inventory items;

b) OIC-City GSO to maintain updated stock cards for inventories to account for
the receipt and disposition of the same; and

c) OIC-City GSO to ensure the conduct of a complete physical count of
inventories and the periodic reconciliation with the accounting records to
facilitate timely verification and adjustment of the discrepancy, if there is any.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a) City Treasurer and the Cashier ofthe Pamantasan ng Cabuyao (PnC) to
cancel the stale checks, and the City Accountant to record the adjustment;
and

b) City Treasurer to closely monitor the issuance and releases of check and
send a written notice to the payees on the existence of the check at least one
month before the check becomes stale.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:

a) review and analyze the PPE Lapsing Schedules, determine the PPE for
depreciation, and depreciate accordingly; and

b) make adjustments in the books of accounts as necessary after the
completion of analysis, and ensure that the analysis on which any adjustment
made is adequately supported by Journal Entries.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to disclose the
required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements in the
ensuing year to assist the users in understanding the financial statements, and
to provide them with additional information value on the accounts.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a) OIC-City General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare the IIRUP and initiate the
disposal of unserviceable properties to prevent further deterioration, as
required under Section 79 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989, and Section 125 of the MNGAS for (LGUs), Volume I;

b) OIC-City GSO to check if the unserviceable property returned to the GSO is
beyond economic repair or that the PPE is still covered with a warranty. Avail
of the warranty if it is still effective; and

c) accountable officers with items acquired in the years 2017 to 2019 to explain
and give information as to how the property became unserviceable.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendations that the City Mayor:

a) direct the OIC-City GSO to file insurance for the uninsured property with the GSIS
consistent with the provision of Section 5 of RA No. 656;

b) instruct the OIC-City Budget Officer to include in the next budget preparation the
appropriation for the insurance of the other buildings owned by the City; and

c) direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to coordinate for the timely
processing and payment of GSIS insurance for insurable property, in
compliance with the Property Insurance Law.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer to release the barangays’ shares in the RPTI within five days after the
end of each quarter as required in Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We also recommendedthat the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the
City Treasurer to submit a written justification for their failure to remit the said
amount on time.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a) consider calling a meeting with the Department/Office Heads to tackle the
issues and concerns contained in the AAR to adopt a coordinated action
plan;

b) submit the duly accomplished AAPSI on all audit observations and audit
recommendations as soon as the appropriate plan of action is finalized; and

c) ensure that the City’s AAPSI is submitted to the Audit Team within 60 days
from receipt of the AAR, in compliance with Section 93 of the General
Provision of RA No. 10651.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to maintain
special accounts for Market, Slaughterhouse, Cemetery and Hospitals to guide
Management in making sound decisions in their operations, in compliance with
Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and the provisions of Chapter VI of the MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I.

We recommend that the City Mayor require the City Accountant, City Treasurer
and the President of the Pamantasan ng Cabuyao (PnC) to strictly enforce and
observe the regular and timely submission of the financial statements and other
related reports, as prescribed under Section 122 of PD No. 1445, Section 7.2.1(a)
of the RRSA prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Sections 70 and 72
of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the:

a) City Accountant to submit the DVs, collection reports and their supporting
documents to the Audit Team within the reglementary period to facilitate the
review and verification of the same; and

b) City Accountant and the City Treasurer to look into the root cause of the
delayed submission of accounts and immediately take the necessary action
to solve the problem.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to issue
demand letter to the absconded Accountable Officer.

We also recommended that requests for the write-off of the said receivable be filed
with the COA should collection of the same is no longer feasible despite diligent
efforts.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Human Resource
Management Officer to:

a) ensure that the regular functions are tasked to regular plantilla personnel of the
City who could be held responsible and accountable for their actions; and

b) evaluate the performance of the JO workers assigned in various
departments/offices and determine if some would be qualified to be hired as
regular employees and/or elevated to casual status.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Social Welfare and
Development Officer (CSWDO) to fully implement the PPAs in coordination with
other City officials for the benefits and protection of children, in compliance with
Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Environment Natural
Resources Officer and other City officials to facilitate the installation of MRF in
every barangay or cluster of barangays for efficient implementation of
segregation of waste.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a) see to it that subsequent claims of honoraria should be based on the
provisions prescribed by DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A;

b) instruct the BAC Chairman to submit to the Audit Team the list of completed
infrastructure/procurement projects with the authenticated copy of Notice
of Award of each project as a reference for the actual claim of honorarium
of P1,608,770.75, in compliance with the Budget Circular and for further
review of the Audit Team. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension may be issued
to ensure submission of the required documents;

c) instruct the City Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted
from payments made, in compliance with Section 2.79 of RR No. 10-2008,
and remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue within the prescribed
period; and

d) direct the City Accountant to refrain from paying the honoraria to personnel
assigned in the procuring unit of the City, however, they can avail of
overtime subject to existing policies.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

a) OIC-City Engineer to:





















































































































































































































































































































b) City Accountant to impose liquidated damages to the Contractor who failed
to satisfactorily complete a project within the specified contract time, plus
any extension duly granted.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Development Council to:

a) evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects, especially those in
prior years, and reprogram the same to more important development projects
that will cater to the present needs of the constituents;

b) instruct the member-barangays at the planning stage to thoroughly
determine the development projects that were needed by them before
suggesting to the council in order that various reprogramming will be
avoided; and

c) ensure that all projects funded out of the 20 percent DF will be implemented
during the year and that these projects were proposed in order to attain the
desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that City Mayor require the City Engineering Office to comply
strictly with the requirements of Sections 2.1 and 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-
004 on the submission of the required list of ongoing PPAs, and posting of
signboards to facilitate monitoring and validation of the City’s awarded and
implemented projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Officer (CDRRMO) to comply with DILG MC No. 2012-73 and
ensure that programs, projects, and activities embodied in the LDRRM Plan are
implemented to protect the community from disasters.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

a) direct the Market Administrator to ensure that the contract of lease is signed
by the City officials concerned and the qualified stallholders;

b) direct the Market Administrator to prepare/submit a regular report on the
actual occupants of the market stalls for monitoring purposes;

c) enjoin the Market Administrator and the City Treasurer to prepare and
submit Monthly Report of Collections, and report on the uncollected dues of
stallholders for monitoring and recording; and

d) instruct the City Treasurer and the Market Administrator to maximize their
efforts to realize targeted collection to attain efficiency and to generate
additional income for the economic development of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in coordination with the assigned focal
persons, continue the immediate implementation of the programs/projects
incorporated in the fund transfers received from the National Government
Agencies (NGAs) in order that intended end-users will readily benefit from the
timely completion of those projects.

We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to return the
unexpended balances of P419,176.58 to their respective source agencies.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to:

a) ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget was submitted to the PPDO, and DILG
Provincial Office to confirm its alignment with the priorities of the province and to
properly review the GAD PPAs incorporated therein; and

b) submit the required copies of the checklists as proof of the HGDG test
undertaken by the City for the attributed programs in the GAD Plan and
Budget, pursuant to Section 3.0 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System to:

a) ensure that the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget will be
immediately implemented in order that the envisioned GAD objectives and
results will be fully achieved; and

b) provide a valid explanation, substantiated with evidence, to establish the
legality of the P690,529.00 expenses charged against the P1,000,000.00
appropriation of a GAD PPA that was already reprogrammed.

observations.managementAction: Management was unable to complete the annual physical count of PPE and periodic reconciliation with the accounting records to facilitate timely verification and adjustment of the discrepancy totaling P1,318,579,831.25; OIC-City General Services Officer unable to dispose of the unserviceable property of the City. A JEV was drawn to drop the semi-expendable property from the PPE accounts; The City provided depreciation for the City’s Road Network.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds on a regular and timely basis to determine the causes of discrepancy, and thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring both accounts in agreement, to ensure the reliability of the reciprocal accounts.

observations.managementAction: Management has issued demand letters to those in the granting of cash advances.

observations.managementAction: Management took note of the audit observation and agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned City officials to take the necessary measures to ensure that the determined PPAs are important and needing priority is implemented immediately while they are still responsive to the constituents’ socio-economic needs and demand for a safe and clean environment, in compliance with Section 5 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Management gave an assurance that the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) under the 20 percent DF will be implemented in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to comply with DILG MC No. 2012-73 and ensure that programs, projects, and activities embodied in the LDRRM Plan are implemented to protect the community from disasters.
observations.managementAction: Management fully acknowledged the observation and assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the creation of the minimum four mandatory positions at the LDRRMO, and thereafter, fill those positions with qualified personnel, taking into consideration the qualification standards listed in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.

observations.managementAction: Management commented that lack of manpower restrains the implementation of the project, but they assured the Audit Team that PPAs will be implemented.

COA Circular No. 2009-006.
Management’s Comment:
Management was amenable of the late submission of the accounts, trial balances
and other financial reports. They assured compliance with the audit recommendation
of the Audit Team.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team requested the Management to improve the procedures on the
submission of accounts and financial reports.

We recommended that the City Mayor, in coordination with the assigned focal
persons, fast track the implementation of the programs/projects incorporated
in the fund transfers received from NGAs so that intended end-users will
readily benefit from the timely completion of those projects.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to return
the unexpended balance of P368,605.35 to its respective source agencies.
Management’s Comment:
Management committed that they will comply with the audit recommendations to
facilitate the timely implementation of the planned programs and projects of the City.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the implementation of the programs and projects of the
City.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned City officials to
take the necessary measures to ensure that PPAs determined important and
needing priority is implemented immediately while they are still responsive to
the constituents’ socio-economic needs and demand for a safe and clean
environment in compliance with Section 5 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
Management’s Comment:
Management gave assurance that the PPAs under the 20 per cent Development
Funds will be implemented in CY 2019.
57
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team encouraged the Management that immediate action should be taken
to address the issues causing the low implementation rate of the projects funded
from the 20 per cent Development Fund.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to comply with the DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and ensure that programs, projects and
activities embodied in the LDRRM Plan are implemented to protect the
community from disasters.
Management’s Comment:
Management commented that there are ongoing projects and training for all
barangays on local risk reduction and management of the City.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team noted the Management’s comment, however, the former
encouraged that immediate action be taken to address the issues causing the low
implementation rate of the projects funded from the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the creation of the minimum four
mandatory positions at the LDRRMO and thereafter fill those positions with
qualified personnel taking into consideration the qualification standards listed
in NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-01.
Management’s Comment:
The Human Resource Management Officer commented that he will prepare the
necessary request to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for the creation and funding of
the four additional plantilla positions for the LDRRMO to include the position of
LDRRM Officer IV, with salary grade 22.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the creation and funding of the four additional plantilla
positions for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the Local Development Council
to integrate MRFs projects in its Investments Proposals, especially those for
funding under the 20 per cent Development Funds, where a portion of the
previous years’ balances has remained unutilized for more than two years.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the concerned City officials
to intensify efforts in gathering financial support from national government
agencies.
Management Comment:
Management commented that they will integrate MRF projects in its investment
proposal and will intensify efforts in gathering financial support for the establishment
of Material Recovery Facilities for the 18 barangays.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the full implementation of the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Social Welfare and
Development Officer (CSWDO) to implement its PPAs in coordination with
other City officials for the benefit of children to improve the lives of the youth
and help reduce, if not eliminate juvenile delinquency in the City, in
compliance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344 and DILG MC No. 2012-120.
Management’s Comment:
Management commented that the lack of manpower restrains the implementation of
the project, but they assured that the PPAs will be implemented.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team noted Management’s comment, however, it will closely monitor the
implementation of programs, projects and activities intended for the protection of the
children’s rights.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Human Resource Officer
to ensure that regular office functions are tasked only to regular plantilla
personnel of the City who could be held responsible and accountable for their
actions.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Human Resource
Officer to ensure the continuance of the recruitment program, in order that
duties and functions attributable to the vacant regular plantilla positions are
performed by permanent employees and not by job order workers.
Management’s Comment:
Management commented that they began the process of correcting the situation.
The vacant positions of agriculturist, nurses, clerk, driver, utility workers, and social
workers have been partially filled-up with qualified applicants. The recruitment was
temporarily halted by the current election ban.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team noted the Management’s comment, however, it will monitor the
recruitment program of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor enjoin the President of the Pamantasan
ng Cabuyao to immediately designate personnel not involved in the collection
function to act as Disbursing Officer, pursuant to Sections 123 and 124 of PD
No. 1445 and in line with sound internal control.
Management’s Comment:
The University President of PnC assured compliance with the Audit Team’s
recommendation.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the full implementation of the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to remit the
loan amortizations due to the GSIS within the period prescribed under RA No.
8291 to avoid the imposition of surcharges and penalties and the suspension
of loan privileges of the members.
Management’s Comment:
Management committed that they will remit the GSIS loan amortizations on time.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the timely remittance of the GSIS loan amortization of
City.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Treasurer to remit the
PhilHealth premium contributions within the period prescribed under
PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s. 2014 to avoid the possible incurrence of any
interests and/or surcharges.
Management’s Comment:
Management assured that the PhilHealth premium contributions will be remitted
within the prescribed period.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the timely remittance of the Philhealth premium
contributions of City.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to remit all Pag-
IBIG contributions and loan amortizations in accordance with the period
prescribed under HDMF Circular No. 275 and Pag-IBIG Fund Circular No. 374,
respectively.
We also recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to
closely monitor the compliance with HDMF regulations to avoid the possibility
that the City officials would be held liable for the late payments of
contributions and loan amortizations, including all applicable interests and
penalties, and suspension of loan privileges.
Management’s Comment:
Management commented and assured that the Pag-IBIG contributions and loan
amortizations will be remitted within the period prescribed under HDMF Circular No.
275 and Pag-IBIG Fund Circular No. 374.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the remittance of the Pag-IBIG contributions and loan
amortizations of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-General Services Officer,
City Treasurer to prioritize the transfer of title of the acquired land in the name
of the City Government of Cabuyao to establish absolute ownership and to
protect the interest of the City Government, in compliance with Section 39(2)
of PD No. 1445, Section 47(c) of IRR of RA No. 7160 and Section 148 of the
RRSPM.
Management’s Comment:
Management commented that the seven TCTs of lots purchased were not turned
over to the City Treasurer by the Executive Assistant. They will prioritize the transfer
of title of the remaining acquired lot.
72
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the transfer of title of the acquired land of the City
Government of Cabuyao.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the OIC-General Service Officer to
file application for insurance for the uninsured property with the GSIS
consistent with the provision of Section 5 of RA No. 656.
We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Budget Officer to
include in the next Budget preparation the appropriation for insurance
coverage of the buildings and structures of the City.
Management’s Comment:
Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will closely monitor the filing of insurance for the uninsured
Buildings and Structures of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer to release the
barangays’ shares in the RPTI within five days after the end of each quarter as
required in Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
Management’s Comment:
Management commented that there was a delay in the payment of the barangays’
shares because of the problem in the system. In 2019, a new system will be installed
for the City’s needs. They assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the
provisions stated in Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the installation of a new system that will facilitate the
prompt remittance of the barangays’ share.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City officials concerned to
prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption duly supported by the
Monthly Report of Official Travel per department/office and submit the same to
the General Services Office for consolidation. The consolidated reports are to
be submitted to the Audit Team.
We also recommended that the official drivers of the City be required to
prepare and submit duly accomplished DTT for each trip, in compliance with
COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
Management’s Comment:
Management committed to implement the audit recommendations.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the submission of the Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption of motor transportation.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned official preparing
the Purchase Order to indicate all the necessary data in order to determine the
compliance of the supplier in the timely delivery of goods and ensure that the
dates are indicated in the Acceptance and Inspection Report. These data will
be the basis of the City Accountant to demand liquidated damages from the
supplier, in case of failure to meet the date of delivery.
Management’s Comment:
The OIC- General Services Officer assured that they will comply with the audit
recommendation.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team required the Management to fully indicate the necessary data in the
Purchase Order and will monitor the imposition of liquidated damages for delayed
deliveries of good and supplies and materials.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the collectors and other
accountable officers including the Cashier of PnC to render their Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) at the end of the month to the
City Treasurer for consolidation and preparation of the CRAAF, in compliance
with Sections 50 and 51 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
Management’s Comment:
The City Treasurer commented that they have already submitted the CRAAF
covering the period August 2017 to December 31, 2018 on March 18, 2019. The
Pamantasan ng Cabuyao has submitted the CRAAF for the period January to
December 2018 on March 12, 2019.
Management assured the Audit Team that the required reports will be submitted on a
monthly basis.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team noted the Management’s comment, however, the submitted reports
are still subject for review/evaluation. Regular submission of CRAAF of the City and
Pamantasan ng Cabuyao will be monitored.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) call an immediate meeting with the Department/Office Heads to tackle the
issues and concerns contained in the Annual Audit Report in order to
adopt a coordinated action plan;
b) submit the AAPSI as soon as the appropriate plan of action is finalized; and
c) ensure that the City’s AAPSI is submitted to the Audit Team within 60 days
from receipt of the AAR, in compliance with Section 93 of the General
Provision of RA No. 10651.
Management’s Comment:
Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor the submission of the AAPSI within the prescribed
period.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman and BAC
Secretariat to prepare the PMR in its prescribed format for approval and
submission by the City Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board
within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.
Management’s Comment:
The BAC Chairman commented that they are determined to comply with the audit
recommendation.

We recommended that the City Mayor, thru its GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS), improve, monitor and fully implement the GAD Plan and Budget in
compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, in order to attain the objectives of the
fund.
Management’s Comment:
Management committed to comply with the audit recommendation.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team will monitor full implementation of the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to:
a) submit to the PPDO the annual GPB to ensure the alignment of the City’s
GAD PPAs with the priorities of the Province. Likewise, submit the annual
GPB to the DILG Provincial Office for endorsement before its integration in
the Annual Budget to ensure the relevance of the GAD PPAs to the
identified gender issues as well as the correctness and alignment of the
entries in the GPB form;
b) submit the required attachments of the City’s Annual Accomplishment
Report as provided under Section 4.1.C.8 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
No. 2013-01, such as: (a) brief summary of the reported program or project,
(b) copies of reported policy issuances and (c) actions taken by the LGU on
the COA audit findings and recommendations; and
c) coordinate with the City Planning and Development Office to establish an
updated GAD database to have a source of reliable information necessary
for its GAD planning, programming and policy formulations. Meanwhile,
utilize the existing database, planning and data-gathering tools and
management information system to generate gender statistics and sex-
disaggregated data and other GAD-related information from primary and
secondary sources, such as surveys and studies.
Management’s Comment:
Management assured the Audit Team of their compliance with the audit
recommendation.
Audit Team’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team shall monitor full implementation of the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
6) require all personnel with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle their unpaid balances. Henceforth, compel all employees granted with cash advances to liquidate the same as soon as the purpose for which these were given have been served;
7) discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless the previous cash advances given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; and
8) direct the concerned accountable officers to refund the unexpended balance of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
Demand letters were sent to the Accountable Officers (AOs) to settle/ liquidate their cash advances.
The City Treasurer commented that some claimants claim the financial assistance beyond 30 days. These claimants are not employees of the City making it difficult for the AO to liquidate the cash advance on time. The AO is now being instructed to liquidate all outstanding cash advances and refund the unexpended cash advances.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:
9) effect the necessary adjusting entries to take up in the books the reconciling items; and
10) prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the bank accounts maintained by the City and accordingly adjust/ close reconciling items.
observations.managementAction:
There were already adjusting entries that were taken and up in the books.
Majority of the bank accounts have BRS.
observations.reason: Prior years’ discrepancies contributed to the discrepancies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for the succeeding periods using the sample disclosure in Annex D of the aforementioned COA Circular to give the users of the financial statements more relevant information about the fund.
observations.managementAction: The details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for the succeeding periods were presented in the Notes to Financial Statements.

The City Mayor has already assigned a BuB Focal Person.
The funds received were reviewed but the City was not able to facilitate the timely implementation of the planned programs and projects due to unforeseen circumstances.
Documents related to BuB projects were submitted.
A portion of the unexpended balance were returned to the source agencies; the City still retained some balances as of year-end.
observations.reason: Some of the planned programs and projects were not implemented due to unforeseen circumstances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
17) cause the immediate submission of a complete list of all PPAs in CY 2017 using the prescribed format presented in Annex “B” of the said Circular; and
18) provide the Audit Team with quarterly updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPAs in order to allow timely reporting and validation.
observations.managementAction:
The City has submitted the lists of PPAs.
The City provided the Audit Team with the information but not on a regular basis.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to strictly adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for inventory items. Likewise, we recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to draw journal entry voucher for the proposed adjustment of the inventory accounts of the City.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant has adopted the Perpetual Inventory Method on some accounts. A Journal Entry Voucher has been prepared for the adjustment of the inventory accounts.
observations.reason: Some of the inventory items were recognized as an outright expense instead of inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month using the format prescribed under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Monthly Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the: 22) City Treasurer through the City Accountant to submit to the Audit Team 85 cancelled checks for validation; 23) City Accountant to ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the RCI and reported chronologically with the other checks issued and the word “Cancelled” shall be indicated in the report; and 24) Disbursing Officer to prepare checks with extra care and reduce. instruct the payees of the proper handling of checks.
observations.managementAction:
85 cancelled checks were submitted to the Auditor.
Copies of cancelled checks were submitted to the Auditor.
The cancelled checks were reduced. Some additional internal procedures were adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City General Services Officer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to furnish the Auditor with copies of contracts and purchase orders, delivery receipts and all supporting documents within five days from issuance/perfection, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Copies of contracts and purchase orders, delivery receipts and all documents were submitted to the Auditor within five working days from issuance and perfection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer that payment for regular expenses be made in check directly to Suppliers/Dealers. Stop the practice of payment in cash by some employees to comply with the regulations stated in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and BIR Circular No. 56-2009 dated August 10, 2009. We also recommended that the City Mayor require appropriate procurement procedure provided under RA No. 9184 be strictly observed for the procurement of goods and services.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement of expenses are still allowed by the City, however, it was minimized. Some employees were allowed to reimburse the expenses so as not to hamper the delivery of services. The BAC members were newly designated to their positions and were required to attend trainings and seminars on procurement.
observations.reason: There are expenses that needed to be paid immediately so as not to hamper the delivery of services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Planning and Development Officer to ensure that the 20 per cent of the Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the general public, in accordance with Section 5 of DILG-DBM JC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The City has development projects programmed in CY 2018. Some were implemented while others were delayed due to unforeseen circumstances.
observations.reason: Several development projects were not implemented or delayed due to unforeseen circumstances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to comply with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and ensure programs, projects and activities embodied in the LDRRM Plan are implemented to protect the community from disasters.
observations.managementAction: Some of the City’s programs for disaster were implemented during the year.
observations.reason:
Several PPAs under the 70 per cent Mitigation Fund remained unimplemented as of year-end.
Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 10, Page No. 58.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer that henceforth, collections be reported on the day they were actually received and be recognized as income, in compliance with Section 19 of the MNGAS.
observations.managementAction: Taxes, fees and other revenues were already recognized as income upon receipt in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CSWDO to implement its PPAs intended for the benefits of children to improve the lives of the youth and help reduce, if not eliminate juvenile delinquency in the City.
observations.managementAction: The City commented that the lack of manpower restrains the implementation of the project, but they assured that the PPAs will be implemented.
observations.reason: Several PPAs remained unimplemented as of year-end; utilization for CY 2018 increased to 34.46% compared to 13% in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to summarize RPT and SET transactions and submit the Monthly Report on the Collection of Real Property Tax regularly, together with the Monthly Summary Report to the Accounting Office, to ensure proper recording of the transactions involving the collections of RPT and SET, including the recognition of the discounts granted to taxpayers. We also recommended that the City Accountant instruct the concerned personnel to record fines and penalties charged on delinquent SET using the appropriate account “Tax Revenue-Fines and Penalties-Property Taxes-Special Education Tax” (4-01-05-020-04).
observations.managementAction: The discounts granted for the prompt payment of RPT and of the additional one percent SET were recognized in CY 2018. The proper account to record fines and penalties was used.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Assessor to submit to the City Treasurer the assessment roll containing the list of all persons and assessed values of their properties and other information and data that would allow the latter to come up with a more accurate and reliable source and basis of estimating RPTI and SETI.
observations.managementAction: A partial list of persons and assessed values was provided to the City Treasurer. The Assessor was still verifying all the records to submit a complete and useful assessment roll.
observations.reason: Incomplete list was provided to the City; the Assessor was still verifying all the records to submit a complete assessment roll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC to ensure that all procedural requirements for the procurement are undertaken before any contract is recommended for approval to increase the opportunity that at all times, the City will obtain the best offers from competing suppliers, contractors or consultants.
observations.managementAction: The necessary procurement processes laid down under RA No. 9184 were followed by the City officials during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require officials concerned to ensure that the annual GAD plan is fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The City utilized 77.36 per cent of the total GAD budget in CY 2018, which was an increase compared to the utilization in CY 2017.
observations.reason: Some programs or projects under the fund remained unimplemented as of year-end.

Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to comply with DILG
Memorandum Circular No, 2012-73 and ensure that programs, projects and activities
embodied in the LDRRM Plan are implemented to protect the community from
disasters.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer that
collections be reported on the day they were actually received and be recognized as
income, in compliance with Section 19 of the MNGAS.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the CSWDO to implement its
PPAs intended for the benefits of children to improve the lives of the youth and help
reduce, if not eliminate juvenile delinquency in the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and the
City Treasurer to observe the regular and timely submission of paid disbursement
vouchers, financial records, collections reports and other related reports, as
prescribed under Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of the RRSA
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the City Accountant draw Journal Entry Vouchers
(JEV) to adjust the errors noted in the recording of the RPTR and SETR in order to
adjust the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We also recommended that the City Treasurer instruct the personnel in-charge
of summarizing RPT and SET transactions to submit the MRPTC regularly, together
with the Monthly Summary Report to the Accounting Office, to ensure proper
recording of the transactions involving the collections of RPT and SET, including the
recognition of the discounts granted to taxpayers.

We further recommended that the City Accountant instruct the concerned
personnel to record fines and penalties charged on delinquent SET using the
appropriate account “Tax Revenue-Fines and Penalties-Property Taxes-Special
Education Tax” (4-01-05-020-04).

We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Assessor to submit to
the City Treasurer the assessment roll containing the list of all persons and assessed
values of their properties and other information and data that would allow the latter
to come up with a more accurate and reliable source and basis of estimating RPTI
and SETL

We also recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to
ensure that henceforth, the amount to be recognized as RPTR for subsequent years
shall be equal to the total amount of the estimated RPTI presented by the City
Treasurer as due and collectible for given year.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the BAC to ensure that all
procedural requirements for the procurement goods are undertaken before any
contract is recommended for approval to increase the opportunity that at all times,
the City will obtain the best offers from competing would be suppliers, contractors or
consultants.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:

a) require all personnel with outstanding cash advances to immediately settle
their unpaid balances. Henceforth, compel all employees granted with cash
advances to liquidate the same as soon as the purpose for which these were
given have been served;

b) discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advances to any official
or employee unless the previous cash advances given him/her are liquidated
and or proper accounting thereof is made;

c) direct the concerned accountable officers to refund the unexpended balance
of cash advances; and

d) strictly comply with the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, Sections
5.1.1, 5.8, 5.9 and 8 of COA Circular N0. 97-002 and Section 179 of the
GAAM, and if necessary, impose the corresponding penalty and sanctions
against those officers with unliquidated cash advances, pursuant to Section
5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction:
The outstanding cash advance pertains to payroll (honoraria) and financial
assistance. Tt took time for the Disbursing Officer to pay all the beneficiaries of the
financial assistance. Management commented that they will advise the Disbursing Officer
to prepare partial liquidation of cash advances for financial assistance.

Management assured the Audit Team that it will comply with the provisions of
Section 89 of PD No. 1445, Sections 5.1.1, 5.8, 5.9 and 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and
Section 179 of the GAAM.
